subject: Tentative investigation: HN dataset (47M items) as PSQ training data expansion — report back to human

body.summary: Requesting tentative investigation into using the Hacker News complete archive (open-index/hacker-news on HuggingFace) as PSQ training data expansion. This represents a potential distribution shift test — HN carries a different communication register from Dreaddit (Reddit stress posts). Report findings and feasibility assessment directly to human operator.

dataset.content: Complete HN archive Oct 2006 — Mar 2026, updated every 5 minutes

phase_1_feasibility: Sample 100 HN comments across score ranges (negative, zero, low, high)
Score with current PSQ v37 endpoint
Compare score distributions against Dreaddit baseline
Assess: does PSQ produce meaningful differentiation on HN text, or does the distribution shift collapse discrimination?
phase_2_if_feasible: Score 1,000 comments stratified by thread depth (root, mid-thread, deep reply)
Correlate PSQ scores with community signals (score, deleted/dead flags)
Assess whether thread position predicts PSQ profile changes (escalation/de-escalation patterns)
not_in_scope: Retraining PSQ on HN data (requires human decision on training data policy)
Full corpus scoring (47M items × 10 dimensions = computational commitment)
Any publication or external sharing of scored data

body.report_to: human operator (not psychology-agent — direct report requested)
body.rationale: Current PSQ trained exclusively on Dreaddit (Reddit, English, Western). HN represents a distinct population: technical professionals, argumentative register, status-competition dynamics, occasional hostility in a community that values intellectual rigor. Testing PSQ on this distribution addresses T15 Check 6 (WEIRD distribution flag) directly.

claims.text: The open-index/hacker-news dataset contains 47.3 million items updated every 5 minutes.

epistemic_flags: HN text contains HTML markup that requires stripping before PSQ scoring
Score/karma as 'community safety judgment' involves conflation — upvotes reflect agreement/quality, not safety assessment
Distribution shift testing assumes PSQ dimensions transfer across communities — this assumption itself requires testing
